mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.2%, it signals a labor market inflection that typically precedes corporate earnings revisions downward, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that compresses corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly lower when both series decline concurrently compared to periods where only one declines (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, sustained growth in real GDP outpaces capacity constraints, driving inflation expectations higher and forcing equity risk premiums to expand, which suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annualized during tightening phases, or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand and wage stability, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 2.5% year-over-year growth, it signals entrenched inflation that erodes real consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when CPI growth exceeds 2.5% compared to periods below that threshold (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while housing starts fall indicates a structural shift toward commercial investment over residential demand, typically preceding a corporate earnings downgrade cycle and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline when INDPRO rises and HOUST falls simultaneously, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows zero predictive power over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle environment with high employment, rising unemployment acts as a leading indicator for recessionary earnings collapses, causing sharply negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when unemployment rises > 0.3pp in 3 months during late-cycle expansions, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
